Create a short Manim animation for a neurodivergent middle-school learner. Concept: Tangent ratio in right triangles. Teach these exact points: Tangent ratio in right triangles: In a right triangle, the tangent of an angle is the ratio of the length of the side opposite the angle to the length of the side adjacent to the angle. Key detail: The tangent ratio (tan theta) is calculated as Opposite/Adjacent, which is used most often because problems typically involve a height (opposite) and a horizontal distance (adjacent). Key detail: Use the tangent (tan θ) ratio when a problem involves the Opposite and Adjacent sides. Use simple labels, large readable text, limited motion, and clear visual cause-and-effect.
